content.intro: Every rental property requires certain compliance certificates to be legally let. PropertyGoose helps you track these certificates and alerts you when they're expiring.

sections.title: Required Certificates
sections.content: The main compliance certificates are: Gas Safety Certificate (annual inspection required), Energy Performance Certificate (EPC - valid for 10 years, minimum rating E required), and Electrical Installation Condition Report (EICR - required every 5 years). Additional certificates may be required based on property type and local regulations.

steps.title: Open the property
steps.content: Go to Properties and click on the property you want to update.

steps.title: Find the Compliance section
steps.content: In the property details, locate the Compliance section.

steps.title: Click 'Add Certificate'
steps.content: Click 'Add Certificate' or the '+' button to add a new compliance record.

steps.title: Select certificate type
steps.content: Choose the type of certificate: Gas Safety, EPC, EICR, or other.

steps.title: Enter dates
steps.content: Enter the issue date and expiry date of the certificate.

steps.title: Upload document (optional)
steps.content: Upload a copy of the certificate for your records.

steps.title: Save
steps.content: Click 'Save' to add the compliance record.

sections.title: Compliance Status Indicators
sections.content: Each property shows a compliance status: 'Valid' (green - all certificates current), 'Expiring Soon' (amber - certificate expiring within 3 months), or 'Expired' (red - certificate has expired). Use the Compliance Status filter to find properties needing attention.

sections.title: Expiry Notifications
sections.content: PropertyGoose sends notifications when certificates are approaching expiry. Make sure to renew certificates before they expire to avoid legal issues.
